questions.q: Which of the following cannot be explained on the basis of Fajan's Rules?
options: Melting point of $\mathrm{AlCl}_3$ is much less than that of NaCl
questions.solution: $\mathrm{BaCO}_3$ is much less soluble than $\mathrm{MgCO}_3$ due to lower hydration energy of $\mathrm{Ba}^{2+}$ ion than that of $\mathrm{Mg}^{2+}$ ion.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Among the following which one will have the largest $\mathrm{O}-\mathrm{O}$ bond length?
questions.solution:
Bond order of $\mathrm{O}_2^{-}, \mathrm{O}_2, \mathrm{O}_2{ }^{+}$and $\mathrm{O}_2{ }^{2-}$ are 1.5, 2.0, 2.5 and 1.0 respectively. Bond length is inversely proportional to bond order.
$\therefore \quad \mathrm{O}-\mathrm{O}$ bond length is largest in $\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{O}_2$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The formation of $\mathrm{NO}^{+}$from NO involves the removal of electron from
questions.solution:
According to MOT
$\begin{aligned}
& \text { NO (15) :- } \sigma 1 s^2 \quad \sigma^* 1 s^2 \quad \sigma 2 s^2 \quad \sigma^* 2 s^2 \quad \sigma 2 p_z^2 \\
& \left(\pi 2 p_x^2=\pi 2 p_y^2\right)\left(\pi^* 2 p_x^1=\pi^* 2 p_y{ }^0\right)
\end{aligned}$

As the last electron is present in $\pi^* 2 p_x$ orbital hence answer will be (1)
